name: WiFi-Pumpkin
use_cases: Use when the MediaTek MT7922 (mt7921e) is in monitor mode on wlan0mon and the BSSID/ssid recon has completed.
Use as a follow-up to airodump / kismet capture when the operator wants to attempt credential recovery or handshake capture.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.

arguments.name: --depth
arguments.description: CLI flag '--depth' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with kimocoder/wifite2 (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with P0cL4bs/WiFi-Pumpkin (score=8); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with morrownr/USB-WiFi (score=7); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'wifi_6_ghz'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'wifi_6_ghz'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'wifi_6_ghz'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'exploit' phase against a target with '['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'wifi_6_ghz']' as the attack surface (wifi, wireless, monitor_mode).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Use when the MediaTek MT7922 (mt7921e) is in monitor mode on wlan0mon and the BSSID/ssid recon has completed.
how_to_use: Step 1: cd toolboxes/WiFi-Pumpkin && python3 WiFi-Pumpkin.py --interface wlan0mon --bssid $KFIOSA_TARGET_BSSID
